Role Description

We are seeking a Learning & Development Manager to own and drive the L&D function end-to-end. This is a hands-on role, responsible for managing existing programs, improving processes, and ensuring measurable impact on employee performance. The ideal candidate will work independently, collaborate with business leaders, and ensure learning initiatives are both strategic and practical. This person will also guide future expansion of the L&D team when required.

Key Responsibilities

  • Take ownership of the L&D function – including planning, execution, governance, and impact measurement.
  • Manage and improve existing training programs.
  • Design and deliver learning content (presentations, playbooks, e-learning modules, assessments) as needed.
  • Ensure effective training delivery, tracking, and evaluation of outcomes.
  • Collaborate with managers to ensure on-the-job application of learned skills.
  • Maintain and update L&D trackers, dashboards, and reports on learning participation, effectiveness, and impact.
  • Manage LMS platforms and learning tools, ensuring smooth operation and accessibility.
  • Support employee development initiatives, onboarding programs, and continuous learning campaigns.
  • Act as the single point of accountability for all L&D initiatives and performance.

Preferred Skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business Administration, Education, or a related field.
  • 4–6 years of experience in Learning & Development, Talent Development, or related roles.
  • Strong hands-on experience in designing and delivering training programs.
  • Experience in managing end-to-end L&D processes and tracking effectiveness.
  • Familiarity with LMS platforms and digital learning tools.
  • Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills.
  • Ability to connect learning initiatives with business outcomes and performance improvement.
  • Experience in technical or software development training is an advantage.
  • High level of ownership, accountability, and execution focus.

About the Role

We are seeking a senior finance leader to oversee the group’s finance function across a dynamic Media, IP-led business. This high-impact role requires combining financial control with commercial judgment, operating strategically and hands-on, and bringing structure to a fast-paced environment. Responsibilities span group finance, cash flow management, budgeting, forecasting, audit compliance, board reporting, multi-entity governance, project finance, and commercial finance support.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead finance function across multiple entities, ensuring governance, reporting integrity, and accounting control.
  • Own cash flow planning, liquidity management, working capital discipline, collections, payables prioritization, and forecasting.
  • Drive budgeting, rolling forecasts, variance analysis, and management reporting with performance and project visibility.
  • Lead statutory closures, audits, tax compliance, and finance preparedness for reviews and filings.
  • Oversee intercompany accounting, cross-border coordination, and consolidation support.
  • Provide financial oversight for project-based operations, including budget review, cost analysis, and profitability evaluation.
  • Prepare board/investor reporting, translating financial data into actionable insights.
  • Lead and mentor finance teams, enhancing accountability and execution discipline.

Preferred Skills

  • Strong experience in financial controllership, cash flow management, budgeting, audit, compliance, and team leadership.
  • Comfort in multi-entity environments, balancing strategic thinking with hands-on execution.
  • Experience in media, entertainment, animation, content production, licensing, or project-driven businesses is advantageous.
